notes: Bronze DQ rules for Gemma 4 AI exposure scores. Grain = bls_soc_code. Expected ~798 rows matching O*NET coverage. Error rows allowed up to 3% of population (per v4 §4 Silver DQ rules — coverage + error rate).

rules.name: Row count >= 780
rules.sql: SELECT CASE WHEN COUNT(*) >= 780 THEN 0 ELSE 1 END AS violation FROM raw_gemma_ai_exposure
rules.description: Gemma should score ~798 O*NET occupations. >=780 allows up to 18 transient failures (~2.3% loss tolerance).
rules.rationale: v4 spec §4 Silver DQ: 'row_count >= 780 (allow <=18 scoring failures / 2.3%)'. Bronze captures both successful and error rows, so count check runs here before Silver drops error rows.

rules.name: Error rate <= 3%
rules.sql: SELECT CASE WHEN SUM(CASE WHEN error IS NOT NULL THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) * 100.0 / NULLIF(COUNT(*), 0) <= 3.0 THEN 0 ELSE 1 END AS violation FROM raw_gemma_ai_exposure
rules.description: Scorer retries 3x before marking a row errored. >3% error rate suggests systematic prompt or model failure.
rules.rationale: v4 spec §4 Silver DQ: 'error non-null <= 3%'. Catches broken scorer config early (wrong model tag, Ollama down).

rules.name: Single model_tag per batch
rules.sql: SELECT CASE WHEN COUNT(DISTINCT model_tag) <= 1 THEN 0 ELSE 1 END AS violation FROM raw_gemma_ai_exposure WHERE error IS NULL
rules.description: All successful scores in a batch must share one model_tag. Mixed tags mean the scorer changed models mid-batch, breaking reproducibility.
rules.rationale: v4 §4 DQ 'single-model-tag-per-batch' + audit-reviewer requirement to trace scores to a specific Ollama tag.

rules.name: No duplicate SOC codes
rules.sql: SELECT bls_soc_code, COUNT(*) AS cnt FROM raw_gemma_ai_exposure GROUP BY bls_soc_code HAVING cnt > 1
rules.description: Scorer produces one row per occupation. Duplicates indicate a checkpoint-resume bug that re-scored an already-completed SOC.
rules.rationale: Grain uniqueness — Bronze is append-only but the scorer is checkpoint-aware. If the checkpoint and output file disagree, we could see duplicates.

rules.name: Rationale length 50-800 chars on successful rows
rules.sql: SELECT * FROM raw_gemma_ai_exposure WHERE error IS NULL AND (LENGTH(rationale) < 50 OR LENGTH(rationale) > 800)
rules.description: Rationale must be 2-3 sentences. <50 chars = truncated, >800 = Gemma ignored the prompt's length guidance.
rules.rationale: v4 spec §4 Silver DQ: 'rationale length 50-800 chars'. Quality signal for downstream Fight AI narrative.
